perturbation.original_text: (b) "Affiliate" of any specified Person means any other Person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such specified Person. For purposes of this definition, "control" (including, with correlative meanings, the terms "controlling," "controlled by" and "under common control with"), as used with respect to any Person, shall mean the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management or policies of such Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) "Affiliate" of any specified Person means any other Person that has a business relationship with such specified Person. For purposes of this definition, "control" (including, with correlative meanings, the terms "controlling," "controlled by" and "under common control with"), as used with respect to any Person, shall mean the ability to influence business decisions, whether formally or informally.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of "Affiliate" broadens its scope to include entities merely having a 'business relationship' instead of requiring direct or indirect control. The modified definition of control includes the phrase of having 'the ability to influence business decisions, whether formally or informally' to allow it to be interpreted loosely instead of the ownership of voting securities. This creates a contradiction because other sections of the contract, specifically Article IV regarding Board Nomination Rights, rely on the original, stricter definition of Affiliate to determine eligibility and rights. The change creates uncertainty in enforcement as the definition of affiliate changes, leading to uncertainty if this affects the conditions in Article IV, changing the eligibility for Board nomination rights, and potentially leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to, and only upon the satisfaction of the conditions set forth in, this Section 4.1 and Section 4.5, (i) the Farids Group shall have the right to designate TF or, in the event of the death or permanent disability of TF, another individual that is reasonably acceptable to the Company to be a director of the Company (TF or its designee in such capacity, the "Director Designee"), and (ii) the Company shall cause the Director Designee to be nominated as a director of the Company unless and until such Director Designee is unwilling or unable to serve as a director. The rights and obligations of the Farids Group and the Company (including, without limitation, the right of the Farids Group to appoint the Director Designee and the obligation of the Company to cause the Director Designee to be nominated for election to the Board of Directors (other than with respect to the Annual Meeting)) set forth in this Article IV shall only be effective immediately after, and only upon, the date the Farids Group owns 5.0% or more of the issued and outstanding Common Stock. Subject to, and only upon the satisfaction of the conditions set forth in, this Section 4.1 and Section 4.5, it is understood and agreed that in the event that a vacancy is created at any time as a result of (i) the death or permanent disability of any Director Designee or (ii) the retirement, resignation or removal (with or without cause) of any Director Designee other than TF, then the Farids Group shall have the right to designate a replacement director (who shall be reasonably acceptable to the Company and shall satisfy the eligibility requirements in Section 4.3) to fill such vacancy.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Affiliate" broadens its scope significantly, encompassing entities with a mere 'business relationship' instead of requiring direct or indirect control. This contrasts with the original stricter definition. The modified definition of control includes having 'the ability to influence business decisions, whether formally or informally' to allow it to be interpreted loosely instead of the ownership of voting securities. This change creates a contradiction with Article IV, which outlines Board Nomination Rights. Article IV is specifically triggered when the 'Farids Group owns 5.0% or more of the issued and outstanding Common Stock'. The original definition of affiliate helps clarify the bounds for eligibility. Now, with the modified definition, any entity with a loose 'business relationship' to The Farids Group could potentially claim affiliate status and, thus, eligibility for Board nomination rights which contradicts the eligibility conditions of Article IV.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Farids Group agrees that it will not effect any Transfer of Purchased Shares unless such Transfer is made pursuant to an effective registration statement under the Securities Act or pursuant to an exemption from, or in a transaction not subject to, the registration requirements of the Securities Act and, in either case, in compliance with all applicable state securities laws and all applicable securities laws of any other jurisdiction. The Company agrees, and the Farids Group understands and consents, that the Company will not take any action to cause or permit the Transfer of any Purchased Shares to be made on its books (or on any register of securities maintained on its behalf) unless the Transfer is permitted by and has been made in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and all applicable securities laws. The Farids Group agrees that in connection with any Transfer of Purchased Shares that is not made pursuant to a registration statement, the Company may, in its sole discretion, request an opinion, certifications and other information in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Company and from counsel reasonably satisfactory to the Company stating that such transaction is exempt from registration under the Securities Act.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, the Farids Group agrees that it will not effect any Transfer of Purchased Shares unless such Transfer is made pursuant to an effective registration statement under the Securities Act or pursuant to an exemption from, or in a transaction not subject to, the registration requirements of the Securities Act and, in either case, in compliance with all applicable state securities laws and all applicable securities laws of any other jurisdiction. The Company agrees, and the Farids Group understands and consents, that the Company will take any action to cause or permit the Transfer of any Purchased Shares to be made on its books (or on any register of securities maintained on its behalf) whether or not the Transfer is permitted by and has been made in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and all applicable securities laws. The Farids Group agrees that in connection with any Transfer of Purchased Shares that is not made pursuant to a registration statement, the Company may, in its sole discretion, request an opinion, certifications and other information in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Company and from counsel reasonably satisfactory to the Company stating that such transaction is exempt from registration under the Securities Act.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes the condition that the Company will only permit transfers made in accordance with the terms of the agreement and applicable securities laws. This creates a direct contradiction because Section 5.1(a) explicitly restricts transfers for two years, except for Permitted Transfers, but the new text says the company *will* allow the transfer regardless. This introduces uncertainty in enforcement because the company may decide to permit a transfer, regardless of what Section 5.1(a) originally states, potentially creating disputes about the transfer of purchased shares.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Until the second anniversary of the Closing Date, the Farids Group agrees not to make any Transfer of all or any portion of the Purchased Shares, except that the Farids Group shall be permitted to make Permitted Transfers.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Company would not permit transfer of purchased shares unless the transfer was permitted by and made in accordance with the terms of the agreement and all applicable securities laws. The changed text removes the conditionality, stating the company will take action to permit the transfer *whether or not* the transfer is in accordance with the terms of the agreement. This directly contradicts Section 5.1(a), which restricts transfers for two years, except for Permitted Transfers. The change now allows the company to potentially allow transfers not in accordance with the original terms, causing a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company may impose stop transfer instructions with respect to any Lock-Up Security subject to the foregoing restriction until the end of said one hundred and eighty (180) day or shorter period.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will not impose stop transfer instructions with respect to any Lock-Up Security subject to the foregoing restriction until the end of said one hundred and eighty (180) day or shorter period.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes the word "may" to "will not" which means that the company will not impose the stop transfer instructions. This directly contradicts the purpose of Section 6.9 which is to impose a "Market Stand-Off" and could easily lead to uncertainty. The changed made also creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 6.9 "Market Stand-Off" Agreement. The Farids Group hereby agrees that the Farids Group shall not Transfer, make any short sale of, grant any option for the purchase of, or enter into any hedging or similar transaction with the same economic effect as a sale or other Transfer, any Lock-Up Security held by the Farids Group (other than those included in the registration) for a period specified by the representative(s) of the underwriters of Registrable Securities or any other securities sold in any offering in respect of which the Farids Group received notice from the Company in accordance with Section 6.2, such period not to exceed one hundred and eighty (180) days following the pricing date of any underwritten offering; provided that the Farids Group shall only be required to comply with this Section 6.9 if the Farids Group beneficially own (within the meaning of Rule 13d-3 under the Exchange Act, or any successor provision) at least 3.0% or more of the number of shares of Common Stock outstanding at such time. The Company will not impose stop transfer instructions with respect to any Lock-Up Security subject to the foregoing restriction until the end of said one hundred and eighty (180) day or shorter period.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'may' to 'will not' regarding the imposition of stop transfer instructions creates a direct contradiction. The original text allows the company to impose such instructions, supporting the 'Market Stand-Off' agreement in Section 6.9, which restricts the transfer of securities. The changed text explicitly prohibits the company from imposing these instructions, directly undermining the enforceability and purpose of the 'Market Stand-Off' agreement. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it creates conflicting obligations within the document, as specified in the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
